observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the Auditor the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels, for evaluation and verification purposes, pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 on the rules and regulations governing the use of government motor vehicles and fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to enforce drivers to properly fill up the driver’s trip tickets.
observations.reason: Driver’s Trip Tickets were not properly filled up, thus, no report being submitted by Management to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to ensure that DTTs are properly and completely accomplished before a vehicle can depart from the garage/motorpool, as provided for in Annex A of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to enforce drivers to properly fill up the driver’s trip tickets.
observations.reason: Driver’s Trip Tickets were not properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare the SEF annual budget for the ensuing fiscal year not later than August 15 of each year in order that the purpose for which the fund was created be realized and planned programs, projects, and activities for the year be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The co-chairman of the local school board committed to prepare the budget for 2023 not later than August 15 this year.
observations.reason: The PPAs of 2022 for the SEF budget were tackled during their meeting on July 2021 but no budget was drafted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Government Officials refund the payment and to stop similar violations in the future.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, in the submission of contracts including their supporting documents to avoid administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445; and in order to implement a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and relevant audit results.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the contractor of the Mamaga-Saclit  FMR to implement, if possible, the noted deficiency or to recover from the contractor the amount of ₱121,112.81 representing the cost of 29.57 cu.m deficiency;
advise the Engineering Office to be very careful in the preparation of their computations and for the Inspectorate Team to include in their inspection activities review all computations before approval to avoid material errors in the presented computations; and
instruct the Engineering Office to conduct further investigations and analysis to determine if the noted observation on the Expansion of Evacuation Center has significant impact on the structure.

We have recommended and Management agreed to update its Revenue Code to improve the revenue generation of the municipality, thereby, more funds will be available in the attainment of the Municipality’s Programs, Projects and Activities, pursuant to Section 191 of R.A. No. 7160.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions  of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the proper use of government vehicles and direct the Supply Officer to mark with the words “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) in the agency’s owned vehicles, under which should be written the name of the Municipality of Sadanga, Mountain Province, to prevent unauthorized use or improper use of the vehicles.
We have recommended and Management agreed to require those persons deemed liable to settle the subject disallowances in accordance with Section 10.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006

We have recommended and Management agreed to include a clause in the purchase order making reference to the general and specific conditions of the contract pursuant to the provisions of the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and 


Henceforth, Management agreed to notify the Office of the auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction:
Purchase Orders were modified to include the necessary information.





The Inspection and Acceptance Committee is notifying the Office of the Auditor when procured goods are delivered.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented | Verification of PO attached to contracts showed that it contains a clause making reference to the general and specific terms and conditions of the contract.

The Audit Team were being notified.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management withhold the mandatory premium contributions of all employees and remit the same on time, to avoid imposition of fine and imprisonment as provided in the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 11223, otherwise known as the Universal Health Care Act.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory premium contributions of employees were withheld and remitted
observations.reason: Latest validation showed that premium contributions of employees were withheld and remitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure the preparation of the Summary/List of Donations and Balances and submit the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnished the COA in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer prepared and submitted the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, require business owners and organizations to submit certified photocopies of their ITRs in compliance with Item e of Section 2E.04, Article E of the Municipal Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury is  strictly implementing the “No filed ITRs, No issuance of Business Permits” that persuaded business owners to file their ITRs.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, coordinate with accredited agencies/trainers for the conduct of and attendance to training of key officials of the LGU to jumpstart its mandatory capacity building program for GAD pursuant to Item 4.1.A.3 of the JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Some officials and employees joined the webinar on Gender Responsive for LGU in June 2021.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Bid and Award Committee Secretariat agreed to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat have prepared the PMR in its prescribed format and submitted to GPPB at the end of the semester.
